data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, evaluating the PLAN AS WRITTEN. ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumptions at the 33d0↔2359 shared-payload seam). Remaining modes read benign.

---
FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload but fixes no canonical label representation; downstream sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) layers a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` COUNT-canary over that payload assuming a uniform integer count, while the per-sub-op signals are NOT uniformly count-shaped (`labels_applied` is list-valued; `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints). The `depends_on 33d0` edge SEQUENCES the work but does NOT decide the label representation, so the shared contract is left unowned at the seam.
- finding: 33d0 (What) "Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path" + its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — fields, comments, labels, links, parent") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Repo-grounded, the typed-leaf candidate payload 33d0 would carry over is asymmetric: `labels_applied` is a `list[str]` of decorated label strings (apply_outbound.py L245/L257/L260/L389) while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325/L278); the existing no-op guard uses TRUTHINESS (`not labels_applied`, L372-378), not `== 0`; and the production batch path (batch_dispatch.py update_one) carries NO per-sub-op counter at all today (applier.py RECON batch_outcome emits only action/key/error), so 33d0's parity gate must MINT the survivor's per-sub-op result shape. Sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied" (an int — apply_inbound.py). 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation for the single survivor, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (behavioral parity — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did, before deletion), 33d0 AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (append `+`/`-` label strings), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int), L372-378 no-op guard (truthiness, not `== 0`); apply_inbound.py inbound `links_applied: int` (the int parity model 2359 cites); batch_dispatch.py update_one (production path applies labels/comments/links, no per-sub-op counter); applier.py RECON batch_outcome (action/key/error only); tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py exact-list label asserts (the tests 33d0 retargets)
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] and links/comments_applied as ints; the existing tests 33d0 retargets assert EXACT label-list contents (not a count); 2359 (depends_on 33d0) layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model; the dual path is repo-confirmed (typed-leaf route typed_dispatch.py L56 / reconcile.py L75 vs batch route batch_dispatch.update_one, whose comment notes production routes through the legacy batch path NOT the typed leaf)
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands the survivor keeping `labels_applied` as a list to satisfy its retargeted exact-list tests → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (`[]` ≠ `0`; a non-empty list is truthy), so the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically even while links/comments are covered — a labels-shaped reinstatement of the bug-3f04 class. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → the exact-list label assertions 33d0 just retargeted break → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) representation left unstated → 33d0's surviving-payload contract and 2359's canary disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly NOT labels) and to parent "full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload label representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op result representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived integer count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so the exact-list tests survive while a count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path. Also leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so 2359 can thread per-sub-op counts in without re-architecting it.

---
SECONDARY (implicit shared state — recorded, the depends_on largely tames it; not separately filed): 33d0 and 2359 co-edit the SAME comment/link/label dispatch region of batch_dispatch.py update_one and the applier.py RECON batch_outcome line. `2359 depends_on 33d0` sequences the work (33d0 lands first), so the residual is only that 33d0 carries no reciprocal note that 2359 will re-touch the same region to add counters/canary, and 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so per-sub-op counts thread in without re-architecting it — folded into the suggested_fix above.

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed):
- Ordering violation — the 33d0→2359 order is captured by `2359 depends_on 33d0` (collapse to one path first, then instrument the survivor). 33d0 also verifies behavioral parity BEFORE deleting the retired path (AC2 gate), so deletion cannot silently drop a capability.
- Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so unlike 822a/59ef (which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate.
- Scope overlap — 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE = consolidation correctness) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) vs 8937 AC3 (snapshot-entry key/shape contract) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→story E/2359) consistently.
- Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; neither edits the applier files, so they are not entangled in 33d0's rewrite.
- Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf's parallel logic AND its now-misleading tests (AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; the dispatch-registry requirement (one callable per valid direction×action) is satisfiable by retaining the (outbound,update) binding as a thin delegator. Siblings 8937/fe57/59ef key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.
